name: Les Adverbes de Quantité — beaucoup, peu, assez

cards.grammarNote: All quantity adverbs are followed by de (never du/de la/des): beaucoup de + noun. No article between de and the noun.
un peu de = a little of. Like all quantity adverbs, followed by de + noun (no article): un peu de fromage, un peu d'eau.
assez de = enough of. assez can also modify adjectives/adverbs meaning 'quite': assez bon (quite good), assez vite (quite fast).
trop de = too much / too many. trop can also modify adjectives: c'est trop sucré (it's too sweet). Always de before the noun, not du/de la.
peu de = little / not much of. j'ai peu de temps = I have little time. Contrast with un peu de (a little) — peu de alone is more negative.

cards.front: I drink a lot of coffee

cards.grammarNote: de elides to d' before a vowel: un peu d'eau, beaucoup d'huile. The rule is the same as with other de constructions.

cards.front: How much wine do you want?
cards.grammarNote: combien de = how much / how many. Also follows the same pattern: combien de + noun (no article).
trop de works for both uncountable (trop de sel) and countable plural nouns (trop de plats). The noun form (singular or plural) follows normal rules.
plein de (informal, = loads of / plenty of) follows the same de + noun pattern. C'est plein de saveur = It's full of flavor. Common in spoken French.
